core_mechanism: continuum percolation / clustering of many randomly placed overlapping elementary sources into merged clusters, where an increasing occupation density drives collective behaviour and the anisotropic spatial geometry of the overlap region imprints a directional (azimuthal Fourier-mode) modulation on the emitted output
brief_reason: The method rests on the 'clustering of color sources' framework in which overlapping sources merge into interacting clusters, and the resulting angular (azimuthal) asymmetry v2 arises from the anisotropic geometry of that clustered overlap region.
